title: Bound lateness with watermarks and allowed lateness
description: Configure watermark delay and allowed lateness to a measured out-of-order bound so windows fire correctly without waiting forever.
body: Use when events can arrive after later-timestamped ones. Do not set a zero watermark delay on an out-of-order source — windows close early and drop late but valid events. Actions: measure the real out-of-order distribution, set the watermark bound to cover the bulk of it, configure allowed lateness for stragglers, and route still-later events to a side output. Validation signals include a dropped-late-record metric near zero under normal skew and a side output capturing genuine stragglers. Risk boundary: too tight a bound silently discards data while too loose stalls results; if lateness exceeds the configured bound regularly, escalate to revisit the source rather than widening unbounded.
